comments.comment: Adding this canvas to the master index to ensure it's cataloged and permanently accessible.
Consider specifying fallback logic if any Lite/Pro variant fails in specific language markets—currently assumes all will succeed uniformly.

The universal translator loop (e.g., Hebrew → English → Optimized → Hebrew) assumes high-fidelity intent translation both ways. You might need to define failover behavior when intent mismatches or cultural context is lost.
Recommend defining clear promotion criteria for experimental variants—what qualifies as 'improvement'? Currently open-ended and subjective.
You should specify frequency and scope boundaries (e.g., max concurrent forks, mutation depth). Without limits, recursive bloat or prompt drift is likely.
The phrase 'Human override option remains in Pro version' implies user governance. Should clarify if override is user-visible or hidden failsafe.
